abstract: We introduce DeepPose, a novel approach to human pose estimation that leverages the power of Deep Neural Networks (DNNs) to reason about body joint localization in a holistic manner. Unlike traditional part-based models that rely on local detectors and constrained structural topologies, our method formulates pose estimation as a direct regression problem. By utilizing a multi-layered convolutional architecture, the model is able to capture complex hidden interactions between joints and incorporate global image context. To improve localization accuracy, we implement a cascade of DNN regressors; an initial stage provides a coarse pose estimate, while subsequent stages refine joint predictions by analyzing higher-resolution sub-images centered around previous estimates. Rigorous empirical evaluation demonstrates that DeepPose achieves state-of-the-art results across several challenging datasets, including FLIC and LSP, effectively handling significant variations in articulation, occlusion, and appearance while maintaining high computational efficiency during inference.
